title: Palm oil
content_html:
Palm oil

A.

Palm oil is an edible oil derived from the fruit of the African oil palm tree, and is currently the most consumed vegetable oil in the world. It’s almost certainly in the soap we wash with in the morning, the sandwich we have for lunch, and the biscuits we snack on during the day. Why is palm oil so attractive for manufacturers? Primarily because its unique properties – such as remaining solid at room temperature – make it an ideal ingredient for long-term preservation, allowing many packaged foods on supermarket shelves to have ‘best before’ dates of months, even years, into the future.

B.

Many farmers have seized the opportunity to maximise the planting of oil palm trees. Between 1990 and 2012, the global land area devoted to growing oil palm trees grew from 6 to 17 million hectares, now accounting for around ten percent of total cropland in the entire world. From a mere two million tonnes of palm oil being produced annually globally 50 years ago, there are now around 60 million tonnes produced every single year, a figure looking likely to double or even triple by the middle of the century.

C.

However, there are multiple reasons why conservationists cite the rapid spread of oil palm plantations as a major concern. There are countless news stories of deforestation, habitat destruction and dwindling species populations, all as a direct result of land clearing to establish oil palm tree monoculture on an industrial scale, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. Endangered species – most famously the Sumatran orangutan, but also rhinos, elephants, tigers, and numerous other fauna – have suffered from the unstoppable spread of oil palm plantations.

D.

‘Palm oil is surely one of the greatest threats to global biodiversity,’ declares Dr Farnon Ellwood of the University of the West of England, Bristol. ‘Palm oil is replacing rainforest, and rainforest is where all the species are. That’s a problem. ’ This has led to some radical questions among environmentalists, such as whether consumers should try to boycott palm oil entirely.

Meanwhile Bhavani Shankar, Professor at SOAS University of London, argues, ‘It’s easy to say that palm oil is the enemy and we should be against it. It makes for a more dramatic story, and it’s very intuitive. But given the complexity of the argument, I think a much more nuanced story is closer to the truth. ’

E.

One response to the boycott movement has been the argument for the vital role palm oil plays in lifting many millions of people in the developing world out of poverty. Is it desirable to have palm oil boycotted, replaced, eliminated from the global supply chain, given how many low-income people in developing countries depend on it for their livelihoods? How best to strike a utilitarian balance between these competing factors has become a serious bone of contention.

F.

Even the deforestation argument isn’t as straightforward as it seems. Oil palm plantations produce at least four and potentially up to ten times more oil per hectare than soybean, rapeseed, sunflower or other competing oils. That immensely high yield – which is predominantly what makes it so profitable – is potentially also an ecological benefit. If ten times more palm oil can be produced from a patch of land than any competing oil, then ten times more land would need to be cleared in order to produce the same volume of oil from that competitor. As for the question of carbon emissions, the issue really depends on what oil palm trees are replacing. Crops vary in the degree to which they sequester carbon – in other words, the amount of carbon they capture from the atmosphere and store within the plant. The more carbon a plant sequesters, the more it reduces the effect of climate change. As Shankar explains: ‘Palm oil production actually sequesters more carbon in some ways than other alternatives. Of course, if you’re cutting down virgin forest it’s terrible – that’s what’s happening in Indonesia and Malaysia, it’s been allowed to get out of hand. But if it’s replacing rice, for example, it might actually sequester more carbon. ’

G.

The industry is now regulated by a group called the Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil (RSPO), consisting of palm growers, retailers, product manufacturers, and other interested parties. Over the past decade or so, an agreement has gradually been reached regarding standards that producers of palm oil have to meet in order for their product to be regarded as officially ‘sustainable’. The RSPO insists upon no virgin forest clearing, transparency and regular assessment of carbon stocks, among other criteria. Only once these requirements are fully satisfied is the oil allowed to be sold as certified sustainable palm oil (CSPO). Recent figures show that the RSPO now certifies around 12 million tonnes of palm oil annually, equivalent to roughly 21 percent of the world’s total palm oil production.

H.

There is even hope that oil palm plantations might not need to be such sterile monocultures, or ‘green deserts’, as Ellwood describes them. New research at Ellwood’s lab hint at one plant which might make all the difference. The bird’s nest fern (Asplenium nidus) grows on trees in an epiphytic fashion (meaning it’s dependent on the tree only for support, not for nutrients), and is native to many tropical regions, where as a keystone species it performs a vital ecological role. Ellwood believes that reintroducing the bird’s nest fern into oil palm plantations could potentially allow these areas to recover their biodiversity, providing a home for all manner of species, from fungi and bacteria, to invertebrates such as insects, amphibians, reptiles and even mammals.

questions.text: examples of a range of potential environmental advantages of oil palm tree cultivation
questions.question: examples of a range of potential environmental advantages of oil palm tree cultivation
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 14–20

Reading Passage has sections A–H. Which section contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A–H, in boxes 14–20 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
environmental advantages
oil palm tree cultivation
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn F: “Oil palm plantations produce at least four and potentially up to ten times more oil per hectare...” và “Palm oil production actually sequesters more carbon...”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Đoạn này nêu nhiều lợi ích môi trường:
cần ít đất hơn
có thể hấp thụ carbon tốt hơn một số cây khác.
=> Đáp án: F

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: 14 examples of a range of potential environmental advantages of oil palm tree cultivation

questions.text: description of an organisation which controls the environmental impact of palm oil production
questions.question: description of an organisation which controls the environmental impact of palm oil production
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
organisation
controls environmental impact
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn G: “The industry is now regulated by a group called the Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil (RSPO)”
Bước 3: Phân tích
RSPO là tổ chức đặt ra tiêu chuẩn sản xuất bền vững.
=> Đáp án: G
questions.question_text: 15 description of an organisation which controls the environmental impact of palm oil production

questions.text: examples of the widespread global use of palm oil
questions.question: examples of the widespread global use of palm oil
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
widespread global use
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn A: “soap we wash with”, “sandwich”, “biscuits”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Đoạn này cho ví dụ palm oil xuất hiện trong rất nhiều sản phẩm hàng ngày.
=> Đáp án: A
questions.question_text: 16 examples of the widespread global use of palm oil

questions.text: reference to a particular species which could benefit the ecosystem of oil palm plantations
questions.question: reference to a particular species which could benefit the ecosystem of oil palm plantations
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
particular species
benefit ecosystem
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn H: “The bird’s nest fern (Asplenium nidus)...”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Bird’s nest fern được cho là có thể giúp phục hồi hệ sinh thái.
=> Đáp án: H
questions.question_text: 17 reference to a particular species which could benefit the ecosystem of oil palm plantations

questions.text: figures illustrating the rapid expansion of the palm oil industry
questions.question: figures illustrating the rapid expansion of the palm oil industry
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
figures
rapid expansion
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn B: “from 6 to 17 million hectares” và “from two million tonnes ... to 60 million tonnes”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Đưa số liệu thể hiện sự tăng trưởng rất nhanh của ngành palm oil.
=> Đáp án: B
questions.question_text: 18 figures illustrating the rapid expansion of the palm oil industry

questions.question: an economic justification for not opposing the palm oil industry

Bước 1: Keyword
economic justification
not opposing
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn E: “palm oil plays in lifting many millions of people in the developing world out of poverty”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Palm oil giúp nhiều người nghèo có sinh kế.
Đây là lý do kinh tế để không tẩy chay ngành này.
=> Đáp án: E

creatures
badly affected
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn C: “Sumatran orangutan ... rhinos, elephants, tigers”
Bước 3: Phân tích
Đoạn này liệt kê các loài động vật bị ảnh hưởng nghiêm trọng.
=> Đáp án: C

questions.text: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Which TWO statements are made about the Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil (RSPO)?
options: Its membership has grown steadily over the course of the last decade.
It demands that certified producers be open and honest about their practices.
It took several years to establish its set of criteria for sustainable palm oil certification.
Its regulations regarding sustainability are stricter than those governing other industries.
It was formed at the request of environmentalists concerned about the loss of virgin forests.
questions.question: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Which TWO statements are made about the Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil (RSPO)?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
open and honest
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn G: “The RSPO insists upon ... transparency”
Bước 3: Phân tích
transparency = minh bạch, công khai.
=> Statement B đúng

questions.text: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Which TWO statements are made about the Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil (RSPO)?
questions.question: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Which TWO statements are made about the Roundtable on Sustainable Palm Oil (RSPO)?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
several years
criteria
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn G: “Over the past decade or so, an agreement has gradually been reached regarding standards”
Bước 3: Phân tích
gradually been reached = mất nhiều năm để thống nhất tiêu chuẩn.
=> Statement C đúng

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 23–25

Complete the notes below.

Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
not refrigerated
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn A: “remaining solid at room temperature”
Bước 3: Phân tích
room temperature = không cần làm lạnh.
=> Đáp án: solid

_blockConfig.noteContent:
• One advantage of palm oil for manufacturers is that it stays [23] even when not refrigerated.
• The [24] is the best known of the animals suffering habitat loss as a result of the spread of oil palm plantations.
• As one of its criteria for the certification of sustainable palm oil, the RSPO insists that growers check [25] on a routine basis.
• Ellwood and his researchers are looking into whether the bird’s nest fern could restore 
[26] in areas where oil palm trees are grown.

questions.questionHtml:
• One advantage of palm oil for manufacturers is that it stays 23_______ even when not refrigerated.
  • The 24_______ is the best known of the animals suffering habitat loss as a result of the spread of oil palm plantations.
  • As one of its criteria for the certification of sustainable palm oil, the RSPO insists that growers check 25_______ on a routine basis.
  • Ellwood and his researchers are looking into whether the bird’s nest fern could restore

26_______ in areas where oil palm trees are grown.
questions.question_text: 23 One advantage of palm oil for manufacturers is that it stays ……………………… even when not refrigerated.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
best known animal
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn C: “most famously the Sumatran orangutan”
Bước 3: Phân tích
most famously = nổi tiếng nhất/được biết đến nhiều nhất.
=> Đáp án: Sumatran orangutan
questions.question_text: 24 The ……………………… is the best known of the animals suffering habitat loss

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
routine basis
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn G: “regular assessment of carbon stocks”
Bước 3: Phân tích
regular assessment = kiểm tra thường xuyên.
=> Đáp án: carbon stocks
questions.question_text: 25 As one of its criteria for the certification of sustainable palm oil, the RSPO insists that growers check ……………………… on a routine basis.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
bird’s nest fern
restore
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
Đoạn H: “allow these areas to recover their biodiversity”
Bước 3: Phân tích
recover biodiversity = restore biodiversity.
=> Đáp án: biodiversity
questions.question_text: 26 Ellwood and his researchers are looking into whether the bird’s nest fern could restore ……………………… in areas where oil palm trees are grown.
